name: receipt-scanning-tools
description: This skill helps you work with the receipt scanning tools in the nonprofit_finance_db project. It includes manual entry tools, automated OCR scanning, and database integration for tracking receipts...
capabilities: Manual receipt entry with categorized merchant selection menus
Database schema documentation for expenses, merchants, and categories
OCR integration patterns using Gemini API for automated scanning
Hierarchical category management with recursive SQL queries
Expense deletion by date range with database operations
Transaction viewing and database inspection commands
author: EGAdams
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing no executable code. All 127 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation patterns (bash examples, configuration placeholders, SQL schema documentation). The skill provides reference material for a nonprofit expense tracking project with no security risks.
valueStatement: Organizations need systematic ways to record and categorize expense receipts for accurate financial tracking. This skill provides documented workflows for manual receipt entry, OCR scanning integration, and database patterns for managing expense records in MySQL.
limitations: This is documentation-only - no direct code execution capability
Requires external MySQL database setup and configuration
Requires virtual environment activation before running tools
OCR features require separate Gemini API key configuration

useCases.title: Track Ministry Expenses
useCases.description: Record and categorize church or ministry expenses including gifts, offerings, and supplies with organized merchant menus.

useCases.title: Manage Receipt Records
useCases.description: Maintain accurate expense records in a MySQL database with proper category hierarchies and merchant tracking.

useCases.title: Build Receipt Tools
useCases.description: Use documented patterns to extend receipt scanning functionality with Python and Rich CLI library.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Start
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I activate the virtual environment and run the receipt tools menu?

promptTemplates.title: Database Schema
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the database schema for expenses and merchants tables.

promptTemplates.title: Add Merchant
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the code pattern for adding a new merchant to the database?

promptTemplates.title: Category Path
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I fetch categories with their full hierarchical path using recursive queries?

bestPractices: Always activate virtual environment before running Python receipt tools
Use context managers for database connections to ensure proper cleanup
Validate user input for dates and amounts before database insertion
antiPatterns: Skipping virtual environment activation leads to ModuleNotFoundError
Assuming column names exist without checking schema first
Not using parameterized queries when inserting user data

faq.question: Which Python version is required?
faq.answer: Python 3.x with mysql-connector-python and Rich library for CLI output.

faq.question: What database does this skill use?
faq.answer: MySQL with a nonprofit_finance database. Connection settings stored in .env file.

faq.question: How do I integrate OCR scanning?
faq.answer: Configure GEMINI_API_KEY in your environment. Use the documented patterns in app/services/receipt_engine.py.

faq.question: Is my financial data safe?
faq.answer: The skill only documents patterns. Database credentials should be stored securely in .env file.

faq.question: Why do I get module import errors?
faq.answer: Most import errors come from forgetting to activate the virtual environment first.

faq.question: How is this different from other receipt tools?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on nonprofit finance with pre-organized merchant categories for ministry giving.
